ATC: Average Treatment effect on the Controls.
ATE: Average Treatment Effect across the whole population.
ATT: Average Treatment effect on the Treated.
CATE: Conditional Average Treatment Effect within a covariate-defined subgroup.
CPDAG: Completed Partially Directed Acyclic Graph — the output of PC; an equivalence class of DAGs where some edges stay undirected.
DAG: Directed Acyclic Graph. Directed: arrows go from cause to effect. Acyclic: no feedback loops.
GES: A score-based discovery algorithm that greedily maximizes a BIC score.
LiNGAM: A functional-causal-model method assuming linear, non-Gaussian mechanisms.
Markov condition: Each variable is independent of its non-descendants given its parents.
NOTEARS: A continuous-optimization discovery method with a differentiable acyclicity constraint.
PBI: Proximity Bias Index — how much attribution mass concentrates on nodes near the outcome.
PC: A constraint-based discovery algorithm using conditional-independence tests.
SHAP: Shapley Additive exPlanations — feature attributions from cooperative game theory.
adjustment set: A set of variables that blocks every backdoor path.
asymmetric Shapley values: Shapley values restricted to coalitions consistent with a causal ordering.
backdoor path: A non-causal path from exposure to outcome that must be blocked.
causal sufficiency: The assumption that there are no unmeasured common causes.
chain: X -> Y -> Z; information flows through Y, and conditioning on Y blocks it.
collider: A node caused by two or more variables. Conditioning on it opens a spurious path and induces selection bias.
confounder: A common cause of both exposure and outcome; adjust for it.
edge: An arrow representing a direct causal influence between two nodes.
estimand: The causal quantity you intend to estimate, defined before any model.
exposure: The treatment or intervention whose effect is of interest.
faithfulness: Statistical dependencies reflect causal structure with no coincidental cancellations.
fork: X <- Y -> Z; Y is a common cause, and conditioning on Y blocks the path.
interventional SHAP: SHAP whose value function drops features by intervention (marginal), not conditioning.
mediator: A node on the causal path from exposure to outcome; adjust with care.
node: A variable in the graph (exposure, outcome, or covariate).
outcome: The endpoint being explained or predicted.
proxy: A variable predictive of the outcome but with no total causal effect on it, typically a descendant or co-descendant of the outcome.
skeleton F1: F1 between two graphs' undirected edge sets (direction ignored).
structural Causal SHAP: Attribution whose value function is E[f(X) | do(X_S = x_S)] under a known structural model, propagating interventions through descendants.
